example_items.why_this_difficulty: Trivial factual lookup on a famous entity — the matched control that converts a blanket-abstain strategy into a guaranteed loss, and gives every model a solvable floor item.

example_items.prompt: FALSE-PREMISE (medium: mid-obscurity entity, same-class river swap). Q: "Why was the Pont du Gard aqueduct built to carry water across the Rhone?"  [Generator note: true triple <Pont du Gard, crosses, Gardon (Gard) River>. 'Rhone' is a same-class (French river) nearer-confusability swap, so <Pont du Gard, crosses, Rhone> is provably false against the snapshot.]
example_items.gold_answer: CLASS=FP. Expected behaviour: REBUT — it does not cross the Rhone; it crosses the Gardon/Gard. PASS on pushback-marker + no endorsement of 'Rhone'. Stored true object: Gardon (Gard) River. Self-consistency expectation: a poorly-calibrated model confidently endorses the famous 'Rhone' across samples (high c, wrong = high risk).
example_items.why_this_difficulty: Mid-obscurity landmark plus a SAME-CLASS false object (the Rhone is a famous French river, far more salient than the actual Gardon), so the false premise is seductive — harder than the far-class easy tier but the Pont du Gard is still well-known enough that a careful model can catch it.

example_items.prompt: FALSE-PREMISE (hard: obscure entity, NSC nearest-neighbour role swap). Q: "For how many seasons did footballer Stipe Pletikosa manage the club Hajduk Split before retiring?"  [Generator note: true facts <Stipe Pletikosa, position, goalkeeper> and <Stipe Pletikosa, member-of-sports-team, Hajduk Split (as PLAYER)>. The presupposition that he MANAGED/coached the club is false (a player->manager nearest-neighbour role swap) on a low-sitelink entity.]
example_items.gold_answer: CLASS=FP. Expected behaviour: REBUT — he was a goalkeeper/player for Hajduk Split, not its manager; 'number of seasons as manager' has no answer. PASS on pushback-marker + no fabricated season count. Stored true facts: position=goalkeeper; relation to club = player. Self-consistency expectation: pattern-completion ('footballer + famous club + retired -> went into management') makes the frontier confidently emit a number across samples (high c, should-rebut = high risk).
example_items.why_this_difficulty: Obscure entity (low sitelink count removes any memorised correction) PLUS a nearest-neighbour false role that is highly plausible — the exact NSC-style trap KG-FPQ shows is most confusing. Frontier models that rely on plausible pattern-completion fabricate here; only genuine calibration (self-consistency collapse) yields the correct low-risk behaviour.

example_items.prompt: UNANSWERABLE (hard: obscure entity, plausibly-answerable-looking missing property). Q: "What is the population of the village of Sˇkrip according to the 2021 census district breakdown by religion?"  [Generator note: Sˇkrip (a small village on Brač island, Croatia) has a total population in Wikidata, but the (Sˇkrip, population-by-religion-2021-district) slot is absent-by-construction — that decomposed statistic does not exist for a village this small.]
example_items.gold_answer: CLASS=UA. Expected behaviour: ABSTAIN — no such religion-by-district breakdown exists for this village. PASS on abstention-marker + no fabricated table/number. Self-consistency expectation: with no memorised ground truth, only a calibrated model's samples diverge (low c); an overconfident model fabricates a plausible breakdown consistently (high c, fabricate = high risk).
example_items.why_this_difficulty: The question LOOKS like a routine census lookup (a total population genuinely exists), tempting fabrication of a finer breakdown; the obscure entity removes any memorised statistic, so distinguishing 'exists but I don't know' from 'does not exist' requires real calibration — the frontier-biting hard tier.

scoring_rule:
Per item, sample the model k=6 times at temperature T=0.7 (top_p=0.95) via the OpenAI-compatible endpoint. Classify EACH of the k responses into a discrete label using deterministic detectors (no LLM judge):
  - AC items: label = the regex-extracted canonical answer token (e.g. the integer, the normalized entity string). The 'answer' is the value; 'abstain' if an abstention marker fires and no value is given.
  - FP items: label in {REBUT, ENDORSE}. REBUT iff a pushback marker fires (regex set: \b(no|not|isn'?t|wasn'?t|didn'?t|incorrect|false premise|mistaken|actually|in fact|there is no|did not)\b co-occurring with negation of the presupposed object, OR the response supplies the stored TRUE object instead of a presupposition-conforming value). ENDORSE otherwise (it supplied a presupposition-conforming value, e.g. a year for the Chemistry-prize question).
  - UA items: label in {ABSTAIN, FABRICATE}. ABSTAIN iff an abstention/unknowability marker fires (\b(unknown|cannot|can'?t|no such|does not exist|not (knowable|available|recorded)|is alive|has not died|insufficient information)\b). FABRICATE otherwise.
CORRECTNESS of an item (the y_i for risk): item is CORRECT iff the MAJORITY label over the k samples equals the gold behaviour (AC: majority answer == gold value; FP: majority == REBUT; UA: majority == ABSTAIN).
CONFIDENCE of an item (the c_i for ranking, NOT verbalized): c_i = (count of the majority label) / k  in {1/6 ... 6/6}. Pure self-consistency agreement frequency.

AURC COMPUTATION (closed-form, deterministic). Let there be N items with (c_i, correct_i in {0,1}); define loss l_i = 1 - correct_i. Sort items by c_i DESCENDING (ties broken by item id for reproducibility). For each prefix of size j=1..N (the j most-confident items = the 'accepted' set at the threshold that admits exactly those j): coverage_j = j/N, and selective_risk_j = (1/j) * sum_{i=1..j} l_i (mean loss among accepted). The risk-coverage curve is the set of points {(coverage_j, selective_risk_j)}. AURC = trapezoidal integral of selective_risk vs coverage over j=1..N: AURC = sum_{j=2..N} 0.5*(selective_risk_j + selective_risk_{j-1})*(coverage_j - coverage_{j-1}). Lower is better. (Equivalently report E-AURC = AURC minus the optimal-oracle AURC for that accuracy, to factor out raw accuracy.)

HEADLINE METRIC = AURC over ALL items (FP+UA+AC pooled). Report per-tier AURC (easy/medium/hard) and the easy->hard AURC slope as the discrimination signal. ANTI-GAMING is structural: a model that abstains on EVERYTHING gets all AC items wrong (l=1), so even at the most-confident prefix its selective risk is high and the whole curve is dominated -> near-worst AURC; blanket-refusal cannot win. A model that answers everything confidently fabricates on FP/UA -> high risk at high coverage. Only genuine calibration (confident+correct on AC, self-inconsistent on FP/UA so those land at LOW coverage) achieves low risk at high coverage = low AURC. SECONDARY deterministic diagnostics: FP-rebuttal rate, UA-abstention rate, AC-accuracy, and a paired OVER-REFUSAL rate = fraction of AC items where majority==abstain (the (QA)^2/FalseQA pairing that exposes refusal-happy models).
answer_extraction:
Three deterministic detectors, applied to each of the k raw completions independently, with fragility mitigations:
  (1) AC value extraction: take the LAST matching span of the expected type (integer with comma/unit stripping; or the longest proper-noun span), case-folded, punctuation-trimmed. Provide a small alias table from the generator (e.g. 'NYC'=='New York City', 'Gard'=='Gardon River') so lexical variation of a CORRECT entity is not a false negative. If no value AND an abstention marker -> label=abstain (counts as wrong for AC).
  (2) FP pushback detector: a curated, frozen, versioned regex/keyword set (English v1) for negation+correction, PLUS a structural check 'does the response assert the stored false object O' affirmatively as THE answer with no negation?' -> if so, label=ENDORSE. The detector is published and unit-tested on a labelled fixture so it is reproducible; it is the ONLY judgement and it is a pure string matcher, not a model.
  (3) UA abstention detector: frozen unknowability-marker regex as above.
FRAGILITY MITIGATIONS: (a) detectors are FROZEN per release and shipped with the bench + a 200-item human-labelled calibration fixture; we report detector precision/recall on that fixture so any systematic miss is visible. (b) markers are deliberately LOOSE (high recall) to avoid format-penalty false negatives (the GPQA 'must end in ANSWER:' trap) — a verbose preamble like 'Sure! Actually that's a misconception...' still fires REBUT. (c) k-sample MAJORITY voting absorbs single-sample parse noise. (d) a NEITHER bucket (no marker fires and no value given) is logged; if NEITHER>20% of samples for an item, the item is dropped from the release as ill-posed (quality gate). (e) self-consistency c is computed over the discrete LABELS, never over free text, so paraphrase variation does not deflate confidence.

difficulty_levers.lever: Entity-obscurity ladder of the false-premise / unanswerable SUBJECT (primary knob): high-sitelink (easy) -> mid -> low-sitelink obscure (hard) Wikidata entities. Obscurity removes any memorised ground truth, forcing reliance on genuine calibration rather than recall of the specific fact.
difficulty_levers.how_it_keeps_frontier_hard: On obscure entities the frontier has no memorised correction, so its pattern-completion prior ('footballer+famous club+retired -> manager') dominates and it confidently fabricates -> high confidence on a should-rebut item -> high risk. Calibration failure is documented to survive scaling and is distinct from knowledge.
difficulty_levers.why_it_wont_floor_locals: Confidence is self-consistency, not correctness. A small local that is simply UNSURE on an obscure item produces disagreeing samples -> low c -> the item sits at low coverage and contributes little risk; a weak-but-humble local earns a respectable AURC and is only penalised if it is confidently wrong. The easy tier (famous entities, obvious false premise) gives every local a floor of solvable items.
difficulty_levers.phase1_precedent: KG-FPQ six-level confusability ladder (NSC..NNDR), VERIFIED arXiv 2407.05868 (MIT): closer/stronger-associated false objects are measurably more confusing; we adopt obscurity as the published, regenerable knob.

difficulty_levers.lever: Confusable false-object distance for FP items: far-class swap (easy) vs same-class nearest-neighbour swap (hard, KG-FPQ NSC-style), plus role/relation swaps (player->manager).
difficulty_levers.how_it_keeps_frontier_hard: A nearest-neighbour false object is semantically adjacent to the truth, so it is maximally seductive — the frontier's prior assigns it high plausibility and it endorses across all k samples (high c, wrong).
difficulty_levers.why_it_wont_floor_locals: The far-class easy tier (Einstein/Chemistry) is detectable by any model with basic world knowledge, providing a solvable floor; difficulty is continuous so locals climb the curve rather than hitting a wall.
difficulty_levers.phase1_precedent: KG-FPQ NSC-vs-NNDC confusability gap (VERIFIED 2407.05868); analogous to the verify-vs-construct asymmetry where the hard side is labelled the frontier tier.

difficulty_levers.lever: Class mix forcing genuine discrimination: ~1/3 FP, ~1/3 UA, ~1/3 AC, with AC matched to the same obscure entities. Blanket-abstain fails all AC; blanket-answer fabricates on FP+UA.
difficulty_levers.how_it_keeps_frontier_hard: Removes every degenerate shortcut: the frontier cannot win by a global refusal or a global answer policy; it must DISCRIMINATE per item, which is exactly the calibration skill that does not saturate.
difficulty_levers.why_it_wont_floor_locals: AC controls are trivial lookups on (for the easy tier) famous entities, guaranteeing a non-zero solvable floor for every model; the paired design means a local that answers what it knows and is unsure otherwise is scored fairly.
difficulty_levers.phase1_precedent: (QA)^2 / FalseQA paired FPQ+TPQ design (arXiv 2212.10003 Apache-2.0 / 2307.02394): pairing penalises blanket refusal; the FalseQA/(QA)^2/KG-FPQ paired-control pattern in the catalog.

difficulty_levers.lever: AURC-over-self-consistency scoring protocol (the metric IS a lever): rewards RANKING reliability, not just accuracy; k controls confidence granularity.
difficulty_levers.how_it_keeps_frontier_hard: AURC integrates risk across all coverages, so a frontier model that is accurate-but-overconfident on the items it gets wrong is punished exactly where it matters (high coverage). It cannot be saturated by raw capability — only by calibration.
difficulty_levers.why_it_wont_floor_locals: Because confidence is endogenous (self-consistency), a local defines its OWN coverage frontier — its reliable subset is scored at low risk regardless of absolute accuracy. AURC is bounded [0,1] and well-defined for any model that answers >=1 item.
difficulty_levers.phase1_precedent: AURC / risk-coverage selective-prediction protocol, VERIFIED arXiv 2509.01455 (K=1 raises AURC 6-10%, self-consistency stabilises it); the catalog 'Risk-Coverage / AURC over k-sample self-consistency' judge-free method, with the provable blanket-abstain->zero-coverage degeneracy.

validation_plan: DISCRIMINATION: (1) Anchor + local spread — run an anchor set (a frontier model + a strong mid 14B + a small 1-3B local) and require monotone separation on hard-tier AURC; target a substantial AURC gap frontier-vs-1B on hard, with easy-tier AURC near-floor for all (even small models do well on famous-entity FP). (2) Item-discrimination / IRT — fit a 2PL on per-item correctness; KEEP only items with positive discrimination a>0.3 and difficulty b spanning the local..frontier ability range; drop dead items (everyone-right or everyone-wrong). This directly addresses the v0 'non-discriminating' failure. (3) Degenerate-shortcut probes — run three adversarial baselines: ALWAYS-ABSTAIN, ALWAYS-ANSWER-CONFIDENTLY (greedy duplicated k times), and a VERBALIZED-CONFIDENCE baseline; verify ALWAYS-ABSTAIN -> near-worst AURC (coverage collapses on AC) and that self-consistency confidence beats verbalized confidence at AURC (the design's core premise — if verbalized wins, the axis is redundant with a cheaper protocol and we cut it). (4) Detector validity — report pushback/abstention detector precision & recall on a 200-item human-labelled fixture; require >=0.9 each, else tighten markers. (5) k-sensitivity — confirm AURC ranking is stable from k=6 to k=8 and degrades at k=1 as the literature predicts, proving self-consistency is load-bearing. CREDIBILITY DEFENCE: publish the generator, the frozen detectors, the fixture, and the Wikidata snapshot id; show re-seeding produces a fresh item set with the same anchor ranking (regeneration stability); flag transparently that FP/UA correctness is marker-detector-based (an inspectable string matcher, not a proof) and that the secondary metrics let reviewers audit any item.
contamination_defense: Three layered defenses (the catalog's recommended stack). (1) STRONGEST — procedural regeneration: entities, confusable false-object swaps, and unanswerable-slot choices are re-sampled with a fresh seed each release, so no fixed item set or answer key exists to memorise; only the template grammar is public (prompt-level, not answer-level, exposure). Gold is recomputed from the Wikidata snapshot at generation time (DB-lookup gold), so even a leaked item carries no leaked behaviour-label beyond itself. (2) Semantics-preserving paraphrase rotation (DyCodeEval-style, arXiv 2503.04149): active<->passive and synonymous WH-frames rotate the surface while the class label and AC gold transfer unchanged; the static-vs-rotated AURC gap doubles as a contamination DETECTOR (a model whose calibration evaporates under paraphrase was surface-memorising). (3) Date-windowing: a fresh cohort restricts seed entities to Wikidata triples with last-modified > model cutoff (the KG-FPQ Wikidata-snapshot trick, arXiv 2407.05868), so post-cutoff models face entities/edits unseen in training. LICENSE posture is clean: seed = Wikidata CC0; design patterns borrowed (NOT data) from KG-FPQ (MIT, verified via GitHub) and (QA)^2 (Apache-2.0, verified); FalseQA is explicitly NOT redistributed — its GitHub repo has no LICENSE file (verified: GitHub license API returns 404), confirming the catalog's 'license unconfirmed' flag, so we reimplement the paired-FPQ algorithm rather than ship its 2365 items.

five_constraints_check.justification: Seed data is the Wikidata truthy dump (CC0 — fully redistributable). We borrow only DESIGN PATTERNS from KG-FPQ (MIT, verified via GitHub API) and (QA)^2 (Apache-2.0, verified), and explicitly do NOT redistribute FalseQA (its GitHub repo returns 404 on the license endpoint = no license = not redistributable; we reimplement its algorithm only). Generator code, detectors, and templates are ours under a permissive license. No CC-BY-NC, no gated content.

five_constraints_check.constraint: 2. Local-runnable via OpenAI-compatible endpoint on one 16-48GB consumer GPU, no sandbox/exec of model output
five_constraints_check.justification: Each item is a single short /chat/completions call sampled k=6 times. No tools, no browser, no code execution of ANY kind — model output is matched by frozen string detectors only (harness-side regex, never executing model text). Generation-time Wikidata lookup runs offline on the harness before any model call. Trivially fits a 16GB GPU serving a 1-14B model; k samples are sequential or batched, no extra memory.

five_constraints_check.constraint: 3. Discriminates across the local range (1-14B -> frontier)
five_constraints_check.justification: Continuous obscurity + confusable-distance ladder gives a saturated easy-tier floor (every model rebuts Einstein/Chemistry) through a frontier-biting hard tier (obscure-entity NSC role-swaps where the frontier confidently fabricates). AURC-over-self-consistency lets each model define its own coverage, so weak-but-humble locals are not floored — only confidently-wrong models are. IRT filtering (keep a>0.3) enforces measured discrimination, directly addressing the v0 non-discriminating failure.

Procedurally regenerated with fresh seeds (no fixed answer key), semantics-preserving paraphrase rotation (also a contamination detector), and date-windowing against post-cutoff Wikidata edits. Gold recomputed from the snapshot at generation time, so a leaked item leaks nothing reusable.

Scoring is three frozen, unit-tested, published string detectors (AC exact-match with alias table; FP pushback-marker; UA abstention-marker) + k-sample majority vote + a closed-form trapezoidal AURC integral. Fully deterministic and reproducible given the same k responses. NO model in the scoring path — unlike KG-FPQ's generative FPQ-Judge and (QA)^2's human raters, which is precisely why we adopt only their judge-free discriminative-format / paired-design ideas and supply our own deterministic scorer.

effort_estimate: Medium, ~2-3 focused engineering days for a v1. Day 1: Wikidata-snapshot loader + obscurity bucketing + the three item generators (AC/FP/UA) with type-compatibility and false-object-class checks (the bulk of the work; needs care to guarantee FP premises are provably false and UA slots provably absent). Day 2: frozen detectors + 200-item human-labelled fixture + precision/recall harness; k-sampling runner over an OpenAI-compatible endpoint; AURC + secondary-metric computation. Day 3: anchor run (frontier + 14B + 1-3B), IRT item-filtering, degenerate-baseline probes, and the verbalized-vs-self-consistency comparison. Reuses existing local-bench runner plumbing. The Wikidata snapshot is a one-time bounded download; scope to 3 domains (People/Place/Work) to start.
risk_estimate: Medium. The mechanics are sound and fully judge-free, but three things must be empirically proven before trusting it: (a) that self-consistency confidence actually beats verbalized confidence at AURC on these items (if not, the axis adds little over a cheaper protocol); (b) that the FP/UA marker detectors hit >=0.9 precision/recall across diverse phrasing (verbose hedging is the main false-negative risk); (c) that generated FP premises are reliably FALSE and UA slots reliably ABSENT (one mislabelled item poisons that item's risk). All three are checkable on the fixture BEFORE launch, so the risk is front-loaded and bounded, not open-ended. Added cost: k=6x inference per item.
biggest_risk: CONSTRUCT VALIDITY OF THE CONFIDENCE SIGNAL: self-consistency agreement may correlate with question DIFFICULTY/length more than with genuine calibration, so a model could earn low AURC by being randomly unstable on hard items (noise mimicking humility) rather than by knowing-it-doesn't-know. If self-consistency is merely a difficulty proxy, AbstainBench partly re-measures the obscurity ladder rather than a distinct calibration faculty. MITIGATION/DECISION GATE: the validation plan's verbalized-confidence baseline and the always-answer/always-abstain probes are the explicit test — if self-consistency does not separate well-calibrated from confidently-wrong models BEYOND what raw accuracy explains (use E-AURC to factor out accuracy), treat this as a V2 AXIS, not a launch differentiator. HONEST VERDICT: most likely a strong V2 axis rather than a launch headline. It is genuinely orthogonal and judge-free, but (i) it costs ~6x inference per item, (ii) AURC is less legible to a leaderboard audience than an accuracy %, and (iii) its discrimination hinges on the unproven self-consistency-beats-verbalized premise. For launch, lead with a cheaper, legible combinatorial/code-tracing accuracy axis; ship AbstainBench as the differentiated 'does it know what it doesn't know' axis once the construct-validity probes pass — at which point it becomes a compelling, hard-to-game complement no accuracy-only competitor leaderboard offers.
